talk_title: Allogeneic CAR Cells: Are They the New Black?

models.name: hospital exemption CD19 CAR‑T cohort
models.genotype_or_key_features: 19 pediatric patients, high‑risk B‑ALL, prior transplant or refractory disease

models.name: GD2 CAR‑T neuroblastoma update
models.genotype_or_key_features: 54 patients (35 trial, 19 exemption), low tumor burden

clinical_data.population: pediatric B‑ALL treated with allogeneic CD19 CAR‑T
neuroblastoma patients with low tumor burden

MOA_summary: Allogeneic CAR‑T cells engineered to lack TCR and HLA molecules (or co‑knockdown CD52) avoid GvHD and host rejection, allowing robust expansion and MRD‑negative complete remissions across B‑ALL, T‑ALL, AML and neuroblastoma; CD7‑CAR‑T utilizes a protein‑expression blocker to target CD7 without fratricide, and GD2‑CAR‑T shows superior outcomes when administered early with low tumor burden.
key_findings: Allogeneic CD19 CAR‑T in 19 high‑risk pediatric B‑ALL patients achieved 100% MRD‑negative CR with a 3‑year PFS of 42% and no grade 3‑4 CRS/ICANS.
Only two cases of acute GvHD occurred (both grade 2, resolved with steroids/JAK1/2 inhibitor) despite use of unmanipulated donor cells.
CD7 CAR‑T with a protein‑expression blocker induced complete MRD‑negative remissions in two pediatric patients, one remaining disease‑free at 2 years post‑allograft.
GD2 CAR‑T in neuroblastoma (n=35) yielded >50% 5‑year EFS in low‑burden disease, with better outcomes when cells were collected at diagnosis.
Pre‑clinical data showed that knocking down TCR/HLA and CD52 together markedly improves expansion and persistence of allogeneic CAR‑T cells.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Two patients relapsed with CD19‑negative blasts, highlighting antigen‑loss escape.
CD7 CAR‑T efficacy limited by low CD3+ cell counts or high blast contamination preventing product generation.
open_questions: What is the optimal lymphodepletion regimen to balance safety and CAR‑T expansion in allogeneic settings?
Can broader antigen targeting (e.g., dual CARs) further reduce relapse due to antigen loss?

talk_title: Allogeneic CAR NK Cell Platforms and Clinical Data

biomarkers.name: LDH
biomarkers.finding: normalization within 2 weeks post‑infusion in a CD70 CAR‑NK patient

biomarkers.name: ctDNA
biomarkers.finding: undetectable after CAR‑NK therapy in a refractory DLBCL patient

models.name: AFM13 + CAR‑NK in Hodgkin lymphoma
models.genotype_or_key_features: 42 heavily pre‑treated pts, median 7 prior lines

clinical_data.n: n=8 (CD70 CAR‑NK patients with HLA‑A3 mismatch)

MOA_summary: Cord‑blood derived allogeneic NK cells are engineered with an IL‑15 armoured CD70‑CAR (or CD5‑CAR) and a safety switch; they expand robustly in patients, persist for months, and mediate tumor cell killing directly and via ADCC, while a bispecific engager (AFM13) redirects NK cells to CD30‑positive Hodgkin lymphoma, achieving >90% ORR.
key_findings: Cord‑blood derived CD70 CAR‑NK cells from optimal units (frozen ≤24 h, nucleated RBC ≈ 80 M) yielded 70% 1‑year PFS and 95% 1‑year OS, whereas delayed‑freeze units performed poorly.
In a 42‑patient Hodgkin lymphoma study combining AFM13 with CAR‑NK, overall response rate exceeded 90% with two‑thirds achieving CR, despite lack of bridging therapy.
CAR‑NK cells persisted up to 6 months in peripheral blood, with early clonal expansion (clone 0) followed by emergence of a memory‑like clone (clone 4) linked to long‑term persistence.
CD5 CAR‑NK therapy in T‑PLL achieved CR in 3 of 4 heavily pre‑treated patients, with donor NK cells tracking to disease clearance.
Manufacturing from a single cord‑blood unit can produce 250 patient doses at <$600 per dose, enabling off‑the‑shelf scalability.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Patients with cord‑blood units frozen after 24 h showed markedly inferior outcomes, indicating product quality as a critical variable.
Limited NK‑cell lifespan (1‑2 weeks) without cytokine support may restrict durability in non‑engineered NK approaches.

combination_strategies.rationale: Redirects NK cells via CD16 to CD30‑positive Hodgkin lymphoma cells
combination_strategies.phenotype: ORR >90%, CR 66% in heavily pre‑treated patients

combination_strategies.rationale: Plurilink NK cells express high‑affinity CD16 to mediate ADCC

open_questions: How does the source of NK cells (cord blood vs peripheral blood vs iPSC) affect long‑term persistence and anti‑tumor immunity?
Can IL‑21 armoring further improve NK‑cell metabolism and durability compared with IL‑15?

talk_title: Allogeneic CAR T Strategies to Overcome Rejection and Enhance Efficacy

biomarkers.name: MRD
biomarkers.finding: Patients with MRD‑positive disease selected for ALO‑501A trial

biomarkers.name: ctDNA reduction
biomarkers.finding: Median 98% ctDNA reduction at day 45 in CAR‑T arm vs 26% increase in observation arm

models.name: LO316 CD70 CAR‑T RCC trial
models.genotype_or_key_features: Renal cell carcinoma, high CD70 expression

clinical_data.population: renal cell carcinoma patients with high CD70

MOA_summary: Allogeneic CAR‑T cells are engineered to eliminate TCR (prevent GvHD) and CD52 (allow anti‑CD52 lymphodepletion), or to express HLA‑E and knock‑out β2‑microglobulin (stealth), enabling robust expansion after standard lymphodepletion; CD70 is leveraged both as a tumor antigen and as a target to deplete alloreactive host T cells, improving persistence and efficacy in RCC and lymphoma.
key_findings: In the MRD‑guided ALO‑501A trial, 7 of 8 patients cleared MRD after a single dose of allogeneic CD19 CAR‑T with standard flu‑cyclophosphamide lymphodepletion, without CRS/ICANS.
CD70‑targeted allogeneic CAR‑T (LO316) achieved a 31% ORR in CD70‑high RCC, with durable responses >12 months in most responders.
Gene‑editing approaches (TCR knockout, CD52 knockout, HLA‑E insertion) enable allogeneic CAR‑T expansion comparable to autologous products despite HLA‑agnostic lymphodepletion.
Selective depletion of alloreactive host T cells via CD70 targeting (the “dagger” approach) enhances CAR‑T persistence and reduces rejection.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: CD70 expression on activated host T cells can cause fratricide; a cis‑masking antibody strategy is used to mitigate this.
Patients receiving anti‑CD52 lymphodepletion showed longer CAR‑T persistence than those without.

combination_strategies.rationale: Selective depletion of host lymphocytes to prevent rejection of CD52‑knockout CAR‑T
combination_strategies.phenotype: Extended CAR‑T persistence up to 2‑3 months
Robust in vivo expansion (Cmax comparable to autologous products)

open_questions: Will dual CD19/CD70 CAR‑T maintain expansion and safety comparable to single‑antigen CARs?
What is the optimal lymphodepletion intensity for MRD‑only settings to balance safety and efficacy?
